name: 工学英語A[電1]
category: 専門教育科目 学部共通
overview: This course is a practical introduction to the language you will need if you get a job as an engineer and will be required to use English at work. It presents English from a wide variety of engineering fields and situations, develops communication skills, covers essential grammar, and provides background in major engineering concepts.
goal: The aim in this course is to aid the development of professional skills based on practical learning. Students will improve their speaking, listening and reading skills, and gain confidence in presenting ideas in English. While improving their speaking, listening and reading skills, students will be able to communicate in work situations with both foreign and domestic engineers, and will be able to present their ideas in English. [International Education, Professional skills based on practical learning, Communication Skills]
outcomes: 国際的な教養、実学に基づく専門能力、コミュニケーション能力
way: Students will engage in a wide range of engineering-related speaking, listening and reading activities, and practice giving explanations of processes to fellow students. The classes will be conducted in English, and students are expected to use only English in class. ノートPC必携。
attention: Students should have passed the four compulsory first-year English courses: English SL I and II, and English RW I and II.
preparation: Students will be asked to preview the vocabulary for the following unit, and review expressions and structures covered in the current lesson. There will be regular homework. 予習（100分）テキストの次回UNITに目を通し、学習内容を確認しながら意味の分からない語句や表現を調べておくこと。また、分からない部分は次回授業で質問できるようにしておくこと。 復習（100分）その日に学習したテキスト本文を音読し、語句や表現、内容の理解を深める。また、宿題に取り組む。UNITの学習ポイントはレポートに応用するのでしっかり把握すること。
evaluation: Participation (35%); Assignments (35%); final presentations (30%). 100点の内訳 = 出席35%; 課題35%;最終プレゼンテーション30%（60点以上で合格）

texts.name: Bonamy, David; Technical English 1 (second edition ) Pearson; ISBN 978-1-292-42446-0
